features: Reset behavior and CSR reset values
Open-drain SCL/SDA drive and loopback behavior
Host mode enable/disable and basic command sequencing
Target mode enable/disable and TX/RX data path
Format FIFO push/pop and status flags
RX FIFO and ACQ FIFO readout
Host timeout configuration and timeout indication
Arbitration loss and bus contention response

directed_tests.name: reset_smoke_csr_map
directed_tests.intent: Apply reset, verify all documented CSRs reset to expected values, confirm RO/WO/RW/W1C access behavior, and sanity-check TL-UL read/write responses across the full register map.

directed_tests.name: open_drain_idle_and_loopback
directed_tests.intent: Verify SCL/SDA are never driven high, output enables control pull-low behavior, and CTRL.line_loopback correctly routes outputs back to inputs for internal self-observation.

directed_tests.name: host_basic_write_sequence
directed_tests.intent: Enable host mode, program timing CSRs with safe values, push a minimal format FIFO command sequence, and confirm start/address/data/stop activity on the I2C pins.

directed_tests.name: host_basic_read_sequence
directed_tests.intent: Exercise a host read transaction, confirm RX FIFO captures returned data, and validate RDATA readout and empty/full status transitions.

directed_tests.name: target_basic_transmit_receive
directed_tests.intent: Enable target mode, program TARGET_ID, preload TX FIFO, emulate an external host transaction, and verify target transmit/receive paths plus ACQDATA readout.

directed_tests.name: fifo_status_and_reset_controls
directed_tests.intent: Fill and drain format, TX, RX, and ACQ FIFOs to observe status flags, then apply FIFO_CTRL resets and confirm levels and empty/full indicators clear correctly.

directed_tests.name: interrupt_state_enable_test
directed_tests.intent: Program INTR_ENABLE, inject interrupts via INTR_TEST, observe INTR_STATE set behavior, and clear selected bits using W1C writes.

directed_tests.name: timeout_and_arbitration_loss
directed_tests.intent: Configure a short host timeout and a forced arbitration-loss scenario to verify timeout/arb-loss status, interrupt generation, and safe bus release.

directed_tests.name: simultaneous_host_target_enable
directed_tests.intent: Enable both host and target modes together, drive bus activity from an external agent, and confirm internal arbitration and role selection do not deadlock.

random_tests.name: tlul_csr_fuzz_sanitized
random_tests.intent: Run constrained-random TL-UL reads/writes over the 19-register map with legal opcodes and byte enables, checking for protocol stability, no X-propagation, and correct RO/WO protection.

random_tests.name: i2c_host_sequence_randomized
random_tests.intent: Generate randomized but legal host command streams through FDATA with varying timing CSR settings, data lengths, repeated starts, and stop conditions to stress host FSM transitions.

random_tests.name: i2c_target_traffic_randomized
random_tests.intent: Randomize target-mode transactions, target address matches/mismatches, TX FIFO payloads, and external master read/write patterns to stress target FSM and ACQ/RX behavior.

random_tests.name: contention_and_bus_noise_randomized
random_tests.intent: Inject randomized external SCL/SDA behavior, including contention, delayed pull-ups, and arbitration edge cases, to validate open-drain robustness and error recovery.

risk_areas.reason: Most likely source of functional bugs due to multiple timing CSRs, format FIFO decoding, and start/stop/address/data sequencing complexity.
Two independent FSMs share the same physical bus, creating high risk of deadlock, incorrect role handoff, or missed bus events.
Four 64-entry FIFOs plus status flags and reset controls create overflow/underflow and off-by-one risks.
W1C semantics, enable masking, test injection, and timeout generation are common integration failure points and easy to regress quickly.
Incorrect drive-high behavior or loopback wiring can break bus interoperability while still passing simple CSR tests.

risk_areas.area: TL-UL access protection and register semantics
risk_areas.reason: RO/WO/W1C behavior, reserved-bit handling, and response correctness must be validated but are lower risk than bus-state logic.

risk_areas.area: Timing CSR programming across speed modes
risk_areas.reason: Programmable timing parameters can expose corner cases, but exhaustive speed characterization is not feasible within the 60-minute budget.
